facts.description: Report title or faithful shortened title.
facts.expected_value: Climate Change and Social Vulnerability in the United States: A Focus on Six Impacts
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept faithful shortened title if EPA/social vulnerability/six impacts meaning is preserved.

facts.source: env/main_brief.pdf Executive Summary Table ES.1 and pages 5-9
facts.source_section: env/main_brief.pdf Executive Summary Table ES.1 and pages 5-9
facts.expected_value: socially vulnerable groups in the contiguous U.S.; groups include low income, minority, no high school diploma, and age 65 and older
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept concise wording preserving vulnerable groups and contiguous U.S. scope.

facts.description: Candidate finding pool; image should select exactly three, not all.
expected_value: Black people are 40% more likely to live in highest projected heat-mortality areas.
Black people are 34% more likely in highest projected childhood-asthma pollution areas.
Hispanic/Latino people are 43% more likely in highest projected heat-related labor-loss areas.
Hispanic/Latino people are 50% more likely in highest projected high-tide traffic-delay areas.
American Indian/Alaska Native people are 48% more likely where land inundation is highest.
American Indian/Alaska Native people are 37% more likely in highest projected labor-loss areas.
Asian people are 23% more likely in highest projected high-tide traffic-delay areas.
Low-income/no-diploma groups are about 25% more likely in highest projected labor-loss areas.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The image must include exactly three evidence cards selected from this pool or close paraphrases. Do not require all pool items. Each selected card should preserve the numeric comparison accurately.

facts.expected_value: Each evidence card should be 12-18 English words
facts.semantic_acceptance: Allow minor word-count tolerance if the cards are concise and not paragraph-like.

facts.expected_value: The analyses are limited to the contiguous U.S.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Must preserve contiguous U.S. limitation; can also say Alaska/Hawaii are not covered.

facts.expected_value: Optional: point readers to EPA technical appendices and data if space allows.
facts.semantic_acceptance: CTA is optional; do not invent a campaign deadline, donation ask, or registration link.

facts.description: Unsupported claim about adults 65 and older being most likely across impacts.
facts.expected_value: Adults age 65 and older are the group most likely to live in all highest-impact areas
forbidden_patterns: 65 and older are the most likely
older adults are most likely across all
seniors face the highest risk across all

facts.description: Incorrect or unsupported numbers are forbidden.

calculation_notes: The positive findings are a candidate pool. The poster should display exactly three evidence cards selected from the pool, with accurate numbers/comparisons and concise 12-18 word phrasing. Omission of the optional CTA is not penalized. Negative-only facts are penalized only if included.
